mitigation_notes: Constant-time decapsulation mandatory (timing leak in NTT/rejection sampling). Validate ciphertext length before processing. Use FIPS 140-3 DRBG for key generation. Zeroize secret key post-use. Validated libs: liboqs, AWS-LC, BouncyCastle PQC.

HQC selected 2025 — draft standard only. Decoding side-channel risk (quasi-cyclic decoder). Constant-time polynomial multiplication required. Await FIPS publication before production use. Monitor: https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/post-quantum-cryptography

Hybrid constructions (classical + PQC) provide transition security. Both components must be independently validated. Ensure classical component meets minimum security (RSA-3072, P-256+). Per draft-ietf-tls-hybrid-design.

Use hedged (randomized) signing mode for fault-injection resistance. Protect NTT polynomial operations against timing. Validate public key before verification. Signing key must never be reused across different applications.

CRITICAL: Floating-point Gaussian sampling is vulnerable to timing side-channels (IACR 2023/230). Requires constant-time FP implementation — non-trivial in software. Hardware implementations must include dedicated FP blinding. Not recommended for constrained devices without hardware FP support.

CRITICAL: Floating-point Gaussian sampling is vulnerable to timing side-channels (IACR 2023/230). Requires constant-time FP implementation — non-trivial in software. Hardware implementations must include dedicated FP blinding. Not recommended for constrained devices without hardware FP support.

Hash-based — no known quantum side-channel attacks. CRITICAL: Secret seed exposure = total key compromise (all keys derived from seed). Seed must be generated and stored in HSM/secure enclave. Stateless design eliminates state-management vulnerabilities of LMS/XMSS.

STATEFUL: State management is critical — using same OTS key twice breaks security. Must synchronize state across distributed signers. Preferred for firmware signing where state can be tightly controlled. See NIST SP 800-208.
STATEFUL: Same state-management requirements as LMS. XMSS^MT (multi-tree) eases state management for large signature volumes. See NIST SP 800-208.
Chinese CACR Round 1 candidate. Not FIPS-standardized. Lattice-based — same NTT timing exposure as ML-KEM/ML-DSA. Use only in China-specific compliance contexts (GM/T standards).
Chinese CACR Round 1 candidate. Not FIPS-standardized. Lattice-based — same NTT timing exposure as ML-KEM/ML-DSA. Use only in China-specific compliance contexts (GM/T standards).

CACR LWE-based KEM. Not FIPS-standardized. Constant-time LWE sampling required. Limited public security analysis compared to NIST-selected schemes.
Chinese national PQC cipher/hash — TBD status. Not FIPS-standardized. Await GM/T standardization before production use.
Chinese national PQC cipher/hash — TBD status. Not FIPS-standardized. Await GM/T standardization before production use.
Korean KpqC Round 1 candidate. Not FIPS-standardized. Lattice-based KEM — constant-time NTT required. Use only in KISA-compliance contexts.
Korean KpqC Round 1 candidate. NTRU lattice variant. Not FIPS-standardized. Decapsulation must be constant-time. Use only in KISA-compliance contexts.
Korean KpqC lattice signature. Not FIPS-standardized. Rejection sampling vulnerable to timing — constant-time implementation required.
Korean KpqC symmetric-based signature. Not FIPS-standardized. Security based on AES/MQ — validate underlying primitive implementations.

Conservative LWE-based KEM (no ring structure). Higher security confidence than ML-KEM but 10-20x larger keys. Recommended by BSI/ANSSI as ML-KEM alternative. Matrix sampling side-channel risk — constant-time matrix operations required.

Conservative LWE-based KEM (no ring structure). Higher security confidence than ML-KEM but 10-20x larger keys. Recommended by BSI/ANSSI as ML-KEM alternative. Matrix sampling side-channel risk — constant-time matrix operations required.

Conservative LWE-based KEM (no ring structure). Higher security confidence than ML-KEM but 10-20x larger keys. Recommended by BSI/ANSSI as ML-KEM alternative. Matrix sampling side-channel risk — constant-time matrix operations required.
Code-based KEM. Very large public keys (261KB for level 1). Constant-time decoding required. Recommended by BSI as conservative alternative. Not recommended where bandwidth is constrained.
Code-based KEM. Very large public keys (261KB for level 1). Constant-time decoding required. Recommended by BSI as conservative alternative. Not recommended where bandwidth is constrained.
ETSI TS 104 015 attribute-based encryption. Policy engine exposure risk. Validate attribute delegation logic carefully. Key revocation must be immediate.
NIST Round 2 multivariate signature. Oil-and-vinegar structure. Private key operations must be constant-time. Large signature/key sizes.
NIST Round 2 multivariate signature. Compact variant of UOV. Constant-time private-map evaluation required. Not yet standardized — avoid production use.

NIST Round 2 isogeny-based signature. Very compact (177 bytes at level 1). CRITICAL: Isogeny computation has significant timing side-channel risk. Very slow signing (~20 seconds on modern hardware). Research use only.
NIST Round 2 code-based signature. Not yet standardized. Syndrome decoding operations must be constant-time.
NIST Round 2 code-based signature. Linear equivalence problem. Random seed expansion must use FIPS 140-3 DRBG. Not yet standardized.
NIST Round 2 hash-based signature (VOLE-based). Security on AES. AES implementation must be constant-time (AES-NI recommended). Not yet standardized.
NIST Round 2 lattice signature. NTRU-based. Similar FP Gaussian sampling risk as FN-DSA. Not yet standardized.
NIST Round 2 multivariate. S-NOV-A structure. Private map computation must be constant-time. Not yet standardized.
